title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Language Lens: Feed Go Canonical Url Identity
summary: Users can submit arbitrary URLs, discovery follows every alternate link, redirects change authority silently, and hostname checks occur only before DNS resolution. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go canonical url identity increment in authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Normalize only justified URI components while preserving distinctions that can identify different publisher resources.
Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Canonical Url Identity · Theory Feed Go Canonical Url Ident…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5660 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-go-canonical-url-identity-17mza2(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_feed_go_canonical_url_identity_17mza2\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?candidates < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted < 1 \|\| admitted >= candidates)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?blockedTargets < 2)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?provenanceRows != candidates)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using normalize and only plus specific case evidence.
